channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Dominican club collapse death toll rises to 226 after woman pulled from rubble dies in hospital
item.description: SANTO DOMINGO, Dominican Republic (AP) — A woman hospitalized after being rescued from the rubble of a roof collapse at a popular nightclub in the Dominican Republic died on Saturday, raising the death toll to 226, health officials said.
item.dc:creator: MARTÍN ADAMES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Attacks on famine-hit camps in Sudan's Darfur leave at least 100 people dead, UN official says
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Sudan's notorious paramilitary group launched a two-day attack on famine-hit camps for displaced people that left more than 100 dead, including 20 children and nine aid workers, in the Darfur region, a U.N. official said Saturday.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Iran and US envoys hold 1st negotiation over Tehran's nuclear program, and talk face-to-face
item.description: MUSCAT, Oman (AP) — Iran and the United States will hold more negotiations next week over Tehran’s rapidly advancing nuclear program, Iranian state television reported Saturday at the end of the first round of talks between the two countries since President Donald Trump returned to the White House.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Netanyahu slams Carney's reply to anti-Israel agitator who said there's a 'genocide' in Gaza
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is demanding Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney "backtrack" his comments after the Canadian leader appeared to agree with a protester who said there's a "genocide" in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia and Ukraine accuse each other of failing to pause strikes after US envoy leaves Moscow
item.description: Russia and Ukraine’s top diplomats on Saturday used a high-level conference in Turkey to once again trade accusations of violating a tentative U.S.-brokered deal to pause strikes on energy infrastructure, underscoring the challenges of negotiating an end to the 3-year-old war.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mexico to send water to Texas amid Trump tariff threats
item.description: Mexico will facilitate an “immediate” delivery of water to farmers in Texas to help shore up resources amid President Trump’s tariff threats, Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum announced on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Serbia's populist president demands order after months of anti-corruption protests shaking his rule
item.description: Serbia’s populist President Aleksandar Vucic has demanded that authorities restore “order and peace” in the Balkan country following months of anti-corruption protests that have shaken his firm grip on power.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: High winds and sandstorms prompt Beijing to cancel hundreds of flights and close parks
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — High winds and the threat of sandstorms Saturday prompted Beijing to cancel hundreds of flights and close public parks, as massive gales felled hundreds of trees, crushing cars and damaging older homes across China’s capital.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Delegates from Iran, US holding talks in Oman amid ongoing tensions: What to know
item.description: Delegates from the United States and Iran are holding talks in Oman Saturday in a delicate effort to restart negotiations over Tehran’s controversial nuclear program.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Majority of polls close in Gabon's first presidential election since a military coup in 2023
item.description: Most polls have closed in the oil-rich Gabon following a presidential election that the country’s military rulers hoped would legitimize their grip on power.
item.dc:creator: Monika Pronczuk And Yves Laurent Goma, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Poland's divisions on display as presidential candidates bicker in a live TV debate
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — Bickering and chaos overshadowed a presidential debate in Poland on Friday evening as eight candidates met to discuss existential matters for this country on NATO's eastern flank — including fears of Russia over its war in Ukraine and concerns about President Donald Trump’s com
item.dc:creator: VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Major nations agree on first-ever global fee on greenhouse gases with plan that targets shipping
item.description: Many of the world’s largest shipping nations decided on Friday to impose a minimum fee of $100 for every ton of greenhouse gases emitted by ships above certain thresholds, in what is effectively the first global tax on greenhouse gas emissions.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER McDERMOTT and SIBI ARASU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Universal announces UK theme park amid 'epic' growth of parks business
item.description: Universal Destinations & Experiences is expanding its global theme park footprint, confirming a new theme park will be built in the U.K. -- the latest in a trio of ambitious projects for the company.
item.dc:creator: Scott Gustin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Prince Harry makes visit to Ukraine, meets with wounded veterans
item.description: Prince Harry is touching down in Ukraine, getting a first-hand look at a rehabilitation center in the war-torn country. The Duke of Sussex visited the Superhumans Center in Lviv on Thursday, a spokesperson confirmed to ITK.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, The Hill

item.title: In a turbulent time, Turkey lays claim to a role of stability for Europe, Syria and global trade
item.description: ISTANBUL (AP) — Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan on Friday sought to position his country as a point of stability in an increasingly tumultuous world, saying Turkey was essential for European security and able to span geopolitical divisions over Ukraine, Syria and U.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW WILKS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Burials begin as the search ends for victims in the Dominican nightclub collapse that killed 221
item.description: SANTO DOMINGO, Dominican Republic (AP) — A merengue icon, a baseball star and others killed when a cement roof collapsed at a popular nightclub in the Dominican Republic were buried Thursday, as authorities called off the search for bodies with the death toll at 221.
item.dc:creator: MARTÍN ADAMES ALCÁNTARA and DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korea establishes diplomatic relations with Syria, a longtime friend of rival North Korea
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea has established diplomatic relations with Syria’s new Islamist government, Seoul’s foreign ministry said Friday, months after a rebel coalition ousted President Bashar Assad, who had maintained close ties with North Korea.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Why Trump and others are worried about the bond market
item.description: President Trump’s tariff plans have raised jitters about the stability of the bond market as demand quickly dropped before he implemented his 90-day pause on most tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, The Hill

item.title: Mexican drug lord convicted in killing of DEA agent Enrique ‘Kiki’ Camarena is freed
item.description: Mexican drug lord Ernesto "Don Neto" Fonseca Carrillo was released from custody after being convicted in the 1985 killing of DEA agent Enrique ‘Kiki’ Camarena.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tariffs on China are 145%, White House clarifies, as stocks slump
item.description: World markets skyrocketed Thursday in the wake of President Donald Trump's 90-day retaliatory tariff pause.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Americans convicted in Congo of a botched coup attempt now face US charges
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — Three Americans repatriated to the United States from Congo this week have been charged with participating in an elaborate coup attempt aimed at overthrowing the African nation’s government last year, the U.S. Justice Department said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Conservative leader Friedrich Merz secures German government coalition after months of political drift
item.description: German's center-right Christian Democratic Union and center-left Social Democrats have reached a deal to form a new German government under Friedrich Merz.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Long, fraught timeline of tensions between Iran and the US as nuclear negotiators meet
item.description: Iran and the United States have held talks in the sultanate of Oman in an attempt to jump-start negotiations over Tehran’s rapidly advancing nuclear program.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Indonesia offers to temporarily shelter wounded and orphaned Palestinians from Gaza
item.description: JAKARTA, Indonesia (AP) — Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto said Wednesday that his country will offer temporary shelter to Palestinian medical evacuees and children orphaned by the Israel-Hamas war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy says 2 Chinese were caught fighting alongside Russia
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — The Ukrainian military has captured two Chinese men fighting alongside the Russian army in the eastern Donetsk region and has information that “significantly more” are with Russian forces, Ukraine’s President Volodymyr Zelenskyy announced on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: VASILISA STEPANENKO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Florida men charged in 'truly heinous' global child porn ring involving over 1M files
item.description: The Florida Department of Law Enforcement arrest seven people in Florida for an international child pornography ring involving over one million files purchased through social media.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US, Panama ‘taking back’ canal from ‘China's influence,’ says Hegseth
item.description: Defense Secretary Hegseth said the U.S. and Panama are strengthening security ties and "together" will "take back" the canal from the influence of China, which operates two major ports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: At least 18 dead, 120 injured in roof collapse at Dominican Republic nightclub
item.description: A roof collapse at Jet Set nightclub in the Dominican Republic killed 18 and injured 120. Rescue teams are searching for survivors, while the cause remains under investigation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 2 Chinese men captured in Ukraine, Zelenskyy claims
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy called for action in the U.S. and Europe after claiming to capture two Chinese nationals fighting in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US withholding water from Mexico to address debt, official says
item.description: The United States is holding back water payments to Mexico in order to send a message, the U.S. International Boundary and Water Commissioner told Border Report.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Duke basketball star could be deported under Trump visa policy
item.description: The State Department announced Saturday that visas of South Sudanese passport holders would be revoked.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says US will deal 'directly' with Iran in high-level meeting on Saturday
item.description: President Trump announced that the U.S. will engage directly with Iran on nuclear negotiations on Saturday though he would not comment on how the talks will differ from JCPOA agreements.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US admiral at NATO fired in expanding national security purge
item.description: U.S. Navy Vice Admiral Shoshana Chatfield is one of only a handful of female Navy three-star officers.
item.dc:creator: Phil Stewart, Idrees Ali, Lili Bayer / Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Yemen's Houthis say US strikes kill 6 as Trump's bombing video suggests higher overall death toll
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Suspected U.S. airstrikes over the weekend targeting Yemen's Houthi rebels killed at least six people, the group said Sunday, while a bombing video posted by U.S.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Thousands of Ukrainian civilians are still held by Russia with uncertain hope of release
item.description: When she heard her front door open almost two years ago, Kostiantyn Zinovkin’s mother thought her son had returned home because he forgot something. Instead, men in balaclavas burst into the apartment in Melitopol, a southern Ukrainian city occupied by Russian forces.
item.dc:creator: DASHA LITVINOVA and HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, WGN 9
